text: There is a sense of gentle chaos, a blur between reality and imagination, like the described hallucinations of subway sounds when things get overwhelming. I feel as though I am teetering on the edge of sense and nonsense, surrounded by conversations and shifting spaces. The world buzzes with energy and confusion, yet I’m oddly comforted—almost as if embracing this confusion is freeing. Being here makes me wonder if meaning is something we build together after the moment has already passed—a shared laugh at odd sensations, or at the unpredictable rhythms of the everyday.
